divergence: The internet was invented by postal workers in 1923
story_angle: deadpan report of a catastrophe that everyone involved considers a great success
headline: Imperial Postal Guild Celebrates Triumph of Total Communication Blackout
deck: Local Officials Declared 'Mail Is Safest When None Is Carried', As Nine-Tenths-Scale Clippers Stand Sentinel.
article:
The Imperial Postal Guild logged the total stoppage of traffic through the Marrow Narrows as an administrative masterstroke. Zero packets moved in the quarter, satisfying every quota by doing nothing. Nine-tenths-scale clippers now sit anchored as fixed compliance monuments.

Lord Postmaster Glimmerfawn stated that correspondence is safest when never attempted. Residents describe an odd calm that follows from receiving nothing. The Guild readies further rules to keep the void intact, with any bypass landing the offender on the unapproved list.
author: Evelyn D. Quillworthy, Senior Scribe of the Guild of Postmasters

weather.condition: Cloudy with a chance of silence

oped.title: Silence Is the Only True Postage: Why the Blackout Deserves Our Thanks
oped.author: Reginald P. Inkless, Perpetual Under-Secretary of the Unmailed and Vice-Chairman of the Feather-Only Correspondence Club
oped.body: The Imperial Postal Guild has performed a public service by declaring mail safest when none travels. One tires of envelopes demanding attention from correspondents whose chief credential is a hat large enough to shelter a pony. These millinery landing stages lure every passing gull. Rather than resume deliveries, extend the blackout and replace all written exchange with one annual semaphore from Guild Hall, performed by me alone so that no one else's trifles intrude. The present arrangement spares us both the weight of bags and the greater weight of other people's opinions.

classifieds.text: For Sale: Entire stock of finest vellum and sealing wafers, now wholly superfluous under the Guild's blackout; desperate seller accepts offers in person at the Old Post Yard, no correspondence entertained.

Help Wanted: Two reliable hands to polish the nine-tenths-scale sentinel clippers daily and deter any unauthorised letter-carrying; steady wages and Guild rations provided, apply before the blackout lifts.
Lost: A bundle of twelve unpaid invoices addressed to Her Majesty's Customs, last seen near the sorting office; finder is entreated to burn them at once lest the Guild discover any mail attempted.
To Miss Arabella S., Mayfair: I remain yours in silence, as the clippers now forbid even a single line; forgive this blackout and know my affection endures though the post does not.

panels.caption: Guild master atop the prow: 'Nine-tenths clippers at every quay—victory at last, not one envelope afloat.'
Bureaucrat to trembling messenger: 'Deliveries are now treason. Burn the sacks before they tempt the wind.'
Messenger, eyeing the flames: 'Then how does the Guild receive its own triumph notice?'

joke.setup: The Imperial Postal Guild, having at last extinguished every letter, wire and dispatch across the realm,
joke.punchline: now congratulates itself on the sole remaining service it can perform without risk of failure: silence.
joke.text: The Imperial Postal Guild, having at last extinguished every letter, wire and dispatch across the realm, now congratulates itself on the sole remaining service it can perform without risk of failure: silence.

sponsor_ads.headline: The Empire's Official Silence Ledger
sponsor_ads.body: Record your forbidden notions upon our indelible vellum, then surrender the volume to Guild couriers who promise it shall never reach its address amid the celebrated blackout.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Tested by the same hands that birthed the aether wires in '23.

sponsor_ads.headline: Pneumatic Tube Extinguishers
sponsor_ads.body: Extinguish rogue dispatches before they breach the blackout; our brass canisters seal messages in wax and deposit them directly into the Thames.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No delivery guaranteed, by order of the Postal Guild.

sponsor_ads.headline: Widow's Blackout Widow's Weeds
sponsor_ads.body: Mourning attire woven with lead thread to muffle any accidental whispers from beyond the veil now that all communication has been triumphantly severed.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Suitable for those who once relied upon the postal aether.

sponsor_ads.headline: The Non-Telegraph Subscription
sponsor_ads.body: Pay yearly for our clerks to intercept and burn any incoming correspondence, ensuring the Guild's blackout remains absolute and your household untroubled by news.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Pioneered by the inventors of universal post in 1923.

stories.headline: Seed Orders Remain Undelivered as Clippers Guard Empty Furrows
stories.deck: Guild Declares Unplanted Acres the Only Blight-Proof Acreage
stories.article:
Farmers watched seed lists disappear into sealed Guild sacks, leaving turned earth bare under the low sun. No catalogues arrive, so nothing is planted and silence counts as protection from written rot.

A Guild foreman noted that empty ground spreads no rumours of failed crops and suggested storing last season's mouldy grain the same way letters are now kept. Extra wax seals have been added to the clippers.

real_world_echo: Mirrors Trump scrapping Hormuz cargo fee while resuming blockade, by having the fee dropped yet the passage sealed with the resulting isolation declared a policy victory
